# Netz

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Sea Level Sunrise

Sunrise calculated as if the location were at sea level, without any elevation adjustment.

This is the sea-level sunrise time. It is used as the basis for dawn calculations that are based on how far the sun is below the horizon before sunrise.

Dawn and dusk depend on the amount of visible light, which is not affected by elevation.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be made, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `AstronomicalCalendar.getSeaLevelSunrise`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.AstronomicalCalendar.getSeaLevelSunrise`

## Sunrise

Sunrise adjusted for the location’s elevation.

The calculation uses a geometric zenith of 90° plus the elevation adjustment, and also accounts for approximately 34 arcminutes of refraction and 16 arcminutes for the sun’s radius, for a total adjustment to 90.83333°.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be made, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `AstronomicalCalendar.getSunrise`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.AstronomicalCalendar.getSunrise`

## Sunrise Or Easternmost Solar Azimuth

Sunrise if it occurs that day; otherwise, in polar regions, the time when the sun is at its easternmost position, azimuth 90°.

If there is no sunrise that day, this uses the time when the sun reaches azimuth 90°. This time is close to six hours before astronomical chatzos hayom, but depending on the time of year and location in the Arctic or Antarctic, it can be up to 46 minutes before or after that time.

This applies to polar regions, the Arctic or Antarctic circles, where there can be days with no sunrise or sunset.

The related sunset opinion uses the sun’s westernmost position, azimuth 270°.

This opinion is attributed to [Rabbi Yehosef Schwarz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Schwarz_(geographer)) in [דברי יוסף – דרך מבוא השמש](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=31703&pgnum=134) and [דברי יוסף – תשובות, שאלה ח׳](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=159&pgnum=83), and is brought down lehalacha by the [Ben Ish Chai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yosef_Hayyim) in [רב פעלים – חלק ב׳, סוד ישרים ס׳ ד׳](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=1401&pgnum=461).

If there is no sunrise that day and the sun does not reach azimuth 90°, this time may not be available.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSunriseOrEasternmostSolarAzimuth`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSunriseOrEasternmostSolarAzimuth`
